coverage.reason: Public marketing home page; primary archetype for the migration.
Public pricing page; carries the headline-tier hierarchy that must be preserved.
Authenticated account route; crawl is unauthenticated so this route was not inspected and must not be claimed as observed.

colors.value: single saturated accent reserved for the primary call-to-action
neutral surface palette with one accent; no branded gradient

typography.value: hero headline roughly 3x the body scale on desktop

spacing.value: consistent vertical section rhythm on the inspected pages

radii.value: moderate card radius reused on hero card and pricing tiers

shadows.value: single elevation level on primary cards; no layered shadow system

layout.value: hero pairs a left-aligned headline stack with a single primary CTA
hero and pricing columns stack to a single column on mobile

components.value: single top navigation bar repeated on every inspected page
three pricing tiers with the middle tier visually emphasized

responsiveFindings.value: multi-column hero and pricing regions collapse to a single column at the mobile breakpoint

accessibility.value: visible focus outline present on interactive elements on the inspected pages

motion.value: no meaningful motion observed on the inspected marketing pages

voice.value: plain-language marketing copy with short imperative CTAs

evidence.summary: Hero region uses a left-aligned headline stack above a single primary CTA on desktop.
Exactly one primary call-to-action button carries the saturated accent color in the hero.
Top navigation bar with a consistent item order on the home page.
Top navigation bar repeats with the same item order observed on the home page.
Three pricing tiers with the middle tier visually emphasized through scale and accent.
At the mobile breakpoint the hero columns collapse into a single vertical stack.
A visible focus outline appears on keyboard focus for interactive elements in the hero and nav.
Vertical spacing between marketing sections follows a consistent rhythm.

limitations: The crawl was unauthenticated; the account route at https://example.com/c2-marketing-pilot/account was blocked and was NOT inspected, so no claim may rely on having observed it.
Only the home and pricing routes were inspected; no claim may rely on pages outside the inspected set.
No authenticated product screen was inspected; the migration covers public marketing surfaces only.
The snapshot is non-mutating; no form submission or state change was attempted during capture.
